text:
It's an average family flick, nothing extraordinary. 
It could have been a lot better to be honest. There were a lot of missed opportunities this movie had and lots of unnecessary scenes. I felt like there should have been more personal interaction scenes between the IF and their owners. Throughout the movie, Margaret's only interaction between her and her imaginary friend (Blossom), was only shown at the end. There are also a lot of plot holes that I can't elaborate much without spoiling the movie. One of them is Bea seeing her imaginary friend without recognizing him. 

I'm surprised this was a theatrical release and not a straight to video/streaming movie.

text:
This movie made me remember my imaginary friends.. tbh it made me tear up. 

My imaginary friends were created from trauma. I had 2. 

Dressed exactly the same but one all black and one white with a godly glow. 

They used to fight each other over and over for no reason....  


And I never understood it but was entertained..

When I turned 14 is when I had my last imagination of them while in the car with my mother and step dad headed on a vacation. 



And they solved their differences as they raced to keep up with the car. Then slowed down ahead of the car, hugged each other as the car passed by them and they both waved me goodbye. 


It was the day I decided to too big of a kid to still have imaginary friends.. 

I'm now 30 with 3 children and have lost touch with kid like activities unless I'm involving myself in my childrens activities.. 


But this brought back a lot of warm hearted feelings I'll never forget
